description: Enable sliding window evaluation (stride=64) by passing compiled_loss_per_token to eval_val calls and reducing val_stride from 256 to 64. The compiled_loss_per_token function was already compiled but never passed, so sliding window eval was effectively disabled. With stride=64, each scored token position has up to 960 tokens of left context instead of 768, giving genuinely better-conditioned predictions and lower BPB at the cost of ~4x more eval windows. This directly fits the eval_time lane: trading evaluation compute for lower val_bpb. (NO CHANGES)

Reduce val_stride from 256 to 64, giving each scored token at least 960 tokens of left context (up from 768) during sliding window evaluation. This trades 4x more eval compute for better-conditioned predictions, directly targeting the eval_time lane's goal of trading evaluation compute for lower val_bpb. The change is a single hyperparameter default; the sliding window evaluation machinery was already active.
